title: Trithala Maha Siva Temple
tagline: Trithala Maha Siva Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Trithala Maha Siva Temple (Malayalam: തൃത്താല മഹാ ശിവക്ഷേത്രം) is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva is situated on the banks of the Bharathappuzha river at Thrithala
overview.description: Trithala Maha Siva Temple (Malayalam: തൃത്താല മഹാ ശിവക്ഷേത്രം) is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva is situated on the banks of the Bharathappuzha river at Thrithala of Palakkad District in Kerala state in India. References to this temple are found in Aithihyamala of Kottarathil Sankunni and many classics of Malayalam Literature. The place where the idol is worshiped in the temple resembles the addition of sand. Legend has it that the Shiva Linga was made by Agnihotri by using the sand from Bharathapuzha river.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Mezhathur
topPlaces.description: Mezhathur is an Indian village in the state of Kerala situated at Pattambi taluk of Palakkad district at the border of Thrissur, Malappuram and Palakkad districts. It is a historical and cultural village in Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Thrithala
topPlaces.description: Thrithala is a town and a village in Pattambi taluk in Palakkad District of Kerala state, South India. The town is located along the banks of Bharathapuzha and is famed for its Shiva temple.

topPlaces.name: Kodumunda
topPlaces.description: Kodumunda is a small village in Muthuthala and Parudur gramapanchayath, Pattambi Taluk, Palakkad district Kerala. It is a minor trading center in Muthuthala Panchayath.

topPlaces.name: Parudur
topPlaces.description: Parudur is a village and gram panchayat in Pattambi taluk, Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India. Parudur was originally a part of Pattambi Block Punjayath in Pattambi Taluk of erstwhile Palakkad district, before the formation of Malappuram district.

topPlaces.name: Kodikkunnu Bhagavathy Temple
topPlaces.description: The Kodikkunnu Bhagavathy Temple or Kodikkunnu Ambalam is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to the goddess Bhadrakali located in the village of Pallippuram, near Pattambi, in Palakkad district of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Kattil Madam Temple
topPlaces.description: Kattil Madam Temple is a dilapidated shrine in the Palakkad district in Kerala, India. It is thought to be a Jain temple built around the ninth or tenth centuries AD and is situated on the Pattambi Guruvayur road.

topPlaces.name: Vavanoor
topPlaces.description: Vavanoor is a village in Pattambi taluk in Palakkad district of Kerala state, south India. The village is located near Koottanad, Pattambi.

topPlaces.name: Muthuthala
topPlaces.description: Muthuthala is a village and gram panchayat. It lies northwest of Pattambi in Palakkad district, of the state of Kerala, India.

The Kodikkunnu Bhagavathy Temple or Kodikkunnu Ambalam is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to the goddess Bhadrakali located in the village of Pallippuram, near Pattambi, in Palakkad district of Kerala, India.

Kattil Madam Temple is a dilapidated shrine in the Palakkad district in Kerala, India. It is thought to be a Jain temple built around the ninth or tenth centuries AD and is situated on the Pattambi Guruvayur road.
Vavanoor is a village in Pattambi taluk in Palakkad district of Kerala state, south India. The village is located near Koottanad, Pattambi.
Muthuthala is a village and gram panchayat. It lies northwest of Pattambi in Palakkad district, of the state of Kerala, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Trithala Maha Siva Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Trithala Maha Siva Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Trithala Maha Siva Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (42 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (93 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Trithala Maha Siva Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Trithala Maha Siva Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Trithala Maha Siva Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Trithala Maha Siva Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Trithala Maha Siva Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mezhathur, Thrithala, Kodumunda, Parudur, Kodikkunnu Bhagavathy Temple.
